original_requirement: As a CAA case officer I want to receive a complete Operational Authorisation pack so that I can open a valid case.
verifiable_rewrite: Pack is complete when all 8 mandatory artefacts (ConOps, SORA, Safety Case, Training Plan, Personnel List, Environmental Assessment, Insurance, Ground Survey) are present, validly formatted (PDF/XLSX/DOCX), and exceed minimum page thresholds: ConOps >= 5 pages, SORA >= 3 pages, Safety Case >= 10 pages.
acceptance_criterion: 100% of submitted packs assessed against 8-artefact checklist; all 8 present + valid format + page thresholds met = GREEN; missing or undersized artefact = RED/AMBER; state transition: complete packs enter open-case, incomplete packs enter return-for-clarification.
reasoning: DeFOSPAM Dorothy-8 identified 'complete' as vague qualifier. Operationalizing through explicit enumeration of 8 required artefacts and numeric page-count thresholds makes 'completeness' testable and repeatable. Acceptance criterion is binary (all 8 + format + thresholds or fail).
recommendation: Require all 20 REQ to enumerate specific artefacts, page counts, or numeric acceptance criteria. Use Vibe meters (METER-001, METER-002) to operationalize compliance scoring.

finding_title: REQ002: 'Swarm-aware' and 'defensibly' undefined; no acceptance criteria
original_requirement: As a CAA SORA assessor I want swarm-aware SORA evidence so that I can set SAIL and OSO requirements defensibly.
verifiable_rewrite: SORA evidence is swarm-aware when it explicitly addresses: (1) Coordinated-link-loss cascades, (2) Emergent misbehavior (deadlock, oscillation), (3) Unintended inter-vehicle interactions. Evidence is defensible when referenced to JARUS SORA v2.5 methodology and documented hazard analysis (FTA or STAMP). Target: >= 3 distinct swarm-aware risk items per aircraft, distributed across 3 categories, with evidence citations.
acceptance_criterion: SORA assessment results in: >= 3 swarm-aware risk items per aircraft, each with evidence citation (test report, FTA, STAMP analysis), all items referenced to JARUS v2.5 methodology, derived SAIL/OSO explicitly reference swarm coordination constraints (e.g., 'Separation >= 50 m horizontal, 20 m vertical during coordinated evasion').
reasoning: DeFOSPAM Dorothy-1, Dorothy-3: 'swarm-aware' is undefined term (confidence 10/10 criticality); 'defensibly' has no criteria for defense. Chris's definition of 'swarm-aware' is 'methodology or evidence accounting for collective behavior.' Operationalizing through 3-component evidence framework (coordinated-link-loss, emergent-misbehavior, inter-vehicle interactions) makes 'swarm-aware' repeatable. 'Defensibly' tied to JARUS v2.5 + documented analysis.
recommendation: Replace subjective terms ('defensibly', 'swarm-aware') with enumerated criteria and methodology references. Require evidence citations (FTA, STAMP, test reports) to tie defense logic to recognized standards.

finding_title: REQ003: 'Clear' and 'judge' are subjective verbs; worst-credible-behaviour undefined
original_requirement: As a CAA safety-case reviewer I want a clear ConOps and ODD so that I can judge nominal and worst-credible behaviour.
verifiable_rewrite: 'Clear' ConOps contains 4 required sections: (1) Swarm composition and roles, (2) Operational phases with crew decision points, (3) Failure modes and contingency responses, (4) Nominal behavior thresholds (e.g., flight plan deviation < 2 degrees, descent rate < 100 ft/min). 'Worst-credible behaviour' is single-failure scenario most severe reasonably foreseeable (loss of GPS, C2 link loss, geofence breach) that system and crew must survive. Validate that all worst-credible scenarios addressed by documented crew procedures or containment systems.
acceptance_criterion: 100% ConOps documents contain 4 required sections; 100% ODD specifies numeric environmental bounds (wind <= 15 knots, temperature -5 to +30°C, GPS accuracy > 5 m, daylight only); >= 90% of credible single-failure scenarios addressed by documented procedures or containment.
reasoning: DeFOSPAM Dorothy-5: 'clear' (no clarity criteria), 'judge' (passive subjective verb), 'worst-credible behaviour' (credibility boundary undefined). Operationalizing 'clear' by enumerating 4 required ConOps sections per Vibe meter METER-002. 'Worst-credible' scoped to single-failure assumption (not common-mode). 'Judge' replaced with 'validate against documented procedures' (testable, binary pass/fail).
recommendation: Replace subjective verbs ('clear', 'judge') with testable actions ('validate', 'verify'). Enumerate specific document sections and acceptance criteria. Define 'worst-credible' scope explicitly (e.g., single-failure assumption).

finding_title: REQ005: 'Automatic containment' undefined; critical safety gap (5 subcriteria)
original_requirement: As a CAA airspace regulator I want automatic containment so that a fly-away cannot become uncontrolled BVLOS.
verifiable_rewrite: Automatic containment = geofence monitor (onboard, 10 Hz polling) + primary containment action (priority: Return-to-Launch if GPS; fallback: climb-and-hold). Upon geofence breach (position > 10 m beyond boundary for > 2 sec), system triggers containment within 500 ms, logs breach, alerts crew within 1 sec. Design validated through HITL simulation (boundary stress testing with +/- 15 m GPS noise) and flight testing (>= 10 flights, deliberate near-breach scenarios).
acceptance_criterion: 100% simulated geofence-breach scenarios trigger containment within 500 ms; 100% breaches logged (timestamp, coordinates); 100% crew alerts within 1 sec; robust operation confirmed under +/- 15 m GPS uncertainty (worst-case); HITL and flight testing pass (zero uncontained breaches in 10 flights).
reasoning: DeFOSPAM Dorothy-4 (5 subcriteria undefined for 'containment'); Observation-1: critical safety gap. 'Containment' lacks design and verification criteria. Operationalizing through 3 design components (monitor, action, alert), specific response times (500 ms action, 1 sec alert), and margin testing (15 m GPS noise). Makes containment testable and verifiable.
recommendation: For all safety-critical requirements, specify design components, response-time budgets, margin factors, and verification approach (simulation + flight test). Tie to performance engineering SLAs (e.g., NFR-LAT-005 for decision latency).

finding_title: REQ006: 'Link loss survivable' undefined on time and action; no failure response
original_requirement: As a CAA spectrum specialist I want authorised monitored C2 and inter-vehicle links so that link loss is survivable and non-interfering.
verifiable_rewrite: 'Link loss survivable' = aircraft and swarm have procedures/design such that loss of C2 or inter-vehicle comms managed without loss of separation or collision. Upon C2 loss (> 1 sec), aircraft executes failover: GPS available -> Return-to-Launch; GPS unavailable -> climb to 500 ft AGL and hold. Survival time (duration crew maintains safe state without ground C2) demonstrated via simulation + flight test; target: >= 5 minutes for BVLOS swarm (crew time to re-establish C2 or independent termination).
acceptance_criterion: 100% EMC tests pass ITU-R power spectral density limits; 100% C2 link-loss scenarios (> 1 sec) trigger failover within 500 ms; 100% failover maneuvers execute correctly (RTL if GPS, climb-and-hold if not); survival time >= 5 min in >= 95% of trials; RSSI/BER telemetry continuous (zero > 2 sec gaps).
reasoning: DeFOSPAM Flo-1, Observation-2: 'Link loss survivable' ambiguous on time and action; 'non-interfering' (interference criteria not specified). Chris defines 'link loss survivable' as 'procedures and design such that loss of comms managed without loss of separation.' Operationalizing through: (1) time threshold (1 sec), (2) failover actions (RTL or hold, specific altitude 500 ft AGL), (3) survival time acceptance (>= 5 min). Closes gap on undefined 'survivable' outcome.
recommendation: For all network/link requirements, specify: link-loss threshold time, failover actions, minimum survival-time duration, and measurement method (simulation + flight test). Tie failover latency to control-loop timing budgets (REQ005).

finding_title: REQ007: 'Resolved predictably' undefined; de-confliction algorithm not specified
original_requirement: As a CAA DAA assessor I want DO-365 / F3442-aligned evidence for each aircraft and coordinated swarm de-confliction so that cooperative and non-cooperative encounters are resolved predictably.
verifiable_rewrite: 'Resolved predictably' = same encounter geometry always produces same response (deterministic, not probabilistic). De-confliction algorithm type specified: (1) centralized ground-based, (2) distributed peer voting, or (3) hierarchical lead-decides. Algorithm computes relative bearing/range, issues maneuver recommendations (climb, descend, left/right turn), enforces agreed maneuver. Cooperative encounters (ADS-B/FLARM intruder): algorithm selects maneuver and enforces agreement. Non-cooperative (no signal): algorithm issues crew-advisory maneuver.
acceptance_criterion: 100% of encounter scenarios produce deterministic response (same geometry = same maneuver); 100% of swarm aircraft execute agreed maneuver within 2 sec in >= 95% of trials; collision avoidance (>= 100 m horizontal separation) maintained in 100% of trials; zero loss-of-separation incidents.
reasoning: DeFOSPAM Paul-1, Observation-3: 'Resolved predictably' is goal without mechanism or acceptance criterion. Chris defines it as 'Conflicts with other aircraft resolved deterministically.' Operationalizing through: (1) 3-option algorithm type enumeration, (2) determinism requirement (same geometry = same response, not probabilistic), (3) 2-sec execution deadline, (4) 100 m separation margin. Acceptance is binary (deterministic response or fail).
recommendation: For all de-confliction/coordination requirements, specify: algorithm type (with options), determinism constraint, maneuver-execution deadline, separation margin, and test coverage (100+ encounter scenarios). Validate against DO-365 and F3442-2 standards.

finding_title: REQ008: 'Decision-making architecture' and 'emergent-behaviour' undefined; no assurance criteria
original_requirement: As a CAA autonomy reviewer I want the decision-making architecture airborne limits and emergent-behaviour evidence so that I can assure what the swarm will do collectively.
verifiable_rewrite: Decision-making architecture must specify one of 3 patterns: (1) Centralized (ground crew or ground-based autonomy decides), (2) Distributed (peer voting), (3) Hierarchical (lead aircraft decides). Documentation specifies decision authority, override rules, communication topology (mesh, star, line), failure modes (leader loss, communication loss, Byzantine aircraft). Emergent-behavior evidence provided through: (1) formal verification (>= 95% state space covered), (2) Monte Carlo simulation (>= 1000 trials per scenario), (3) HITL testing. Each scenario bounds behavior variance (e.g., 'separation loss < 0.1% of trials').
acceptance_criterion: 100% of architectures specify decision authority, communication topology, failure modes; formal verification covers >= 95% of state space; Monte Carlo bounds variance to <= 0.1% for safety-critical behaviors (separation loss, deadlock); HITL testing shows >= 95% within predicted behavior envelope.
reasoning: DeFOSPAM: 'Decision-making architecture' requires assurance but does not specify architecture options. 'Emergent-behaviour' undefined (desired vs. undesired). Chris defines 'decision-making architecture' as 'Design of how collective decisions made.' Operationalizing through: (1) 3-pattern enumeration, (2) failure-mode analysis, (3) 3-tier evidence (formal + simulation + HITL). 'Emergent behavior' operationalized through variance bounding (0.1% for safety-critical).
recommendation: For all swarm-coordination requirements, enumerate architecture patterns and require: (1) formal verification with state-space coverage metric, (2) Monte Carlo simulation with variance bounds for safety-critical behaviors, (3) HITL testing with scenario coverage. Use confidence/credibility scoring per Vibe meter guidelines.

finding_title: REQ009: 'Forensically explainable' unrealistic; 'frozen-model manifest' undefined (7 subcriteria)
original_requirement: As a CAA AI/ML assurance specialist I want an AMLAS / SACE case a frozen-model manifest and explainability evidence so that ML components are assurable and forensically explainable.
verifiable_rewrite: 'Forensically explainable' = sufficient for investigation, not perfect reconstruction (deep-learning post-hoc explainability infeasible). Explainability demonstrated through: (1) feature-importance analysis (SHAP, LIME), (2) decision-tree approximations, (3) mechanistic interpretability. Frozen-Model Manifest specifies per model: version ID, training-dataset hash (SHA256), hyperparameters, performance metrics (validation accuracy, F1 score), operational constraints (input domain, confidence thresholds), explicit confirmation model weights/architecture immutable throughout trial.
acceptance_criterion: 100% AMLAS cases complete per Assure ML CTC (safety requirements, training-data governance, model architecture, robustness testing, failure modes all documented); 100% Frozen-Model Manifests immutable (SHA256 verification + code review); 100% of models have >= 2 deployed explainability methods; forensic explainability demonstrated: feature attribution documented, training-distribution verification possible, model-version tracing enabled for 100% of decision instances.
reasoning: DeFOSPAM Dorothy-6, Dorothy-7: 'forensically explainable' is undefined and unrealistic (perfect reconstruction infeasible for deep learning). 'Frozen-model manifest' has 7 undefined subcriteria. Chris defines 'forensically explainable' as 'reconstructable through model weights, training data, decision logic.' Reframing as 'sufficient for investigation, not perfect reconstruction.' Operationalizing 'frozen-model manifest' through 7-field specification (version, dataset hash, hyperparameters, metrics, constraints, immutability, hash verification).
recommendation: For all ML requirements, replace 'perfect explainability' with 'sufficient for investigation' standard. Require Frozen-Model Manifest with 7+ fields (version, hash, hyperparameters, metrics, constraints, immutability confirmation). Mandate >= 2 explainability techniques (SHAP + LIME, or LIME + mechanistic). Tie to AMLAS CTC checklist.

finding_title: REQ010: 'Meaningful human control' undefined; 'throughout' temporal ambiguity; workload thresholds missing
original_requirement: As a CAA human factors inspector I want workload duty and take-over evidence so that meaningful human control is demonstrated throughout.
verifiable_rewrite: 'Meaningful human control' = (1) continuous crew situational awareness (>= 1 Hz updates per aircraft), (2) retained authority (override latency < 2 sec), (3) intervention capability (time window >= 5-10 sec for evasion/return-to-launch). Workload quantified per flight phase (nominal <= 75% cognitive capacity, contingency <= 85%). Takeover demonstrated through: (A) time-to-takeover (VLOS <= 5 sec, BVLOS single <= 10 sec, BVLOS swarm <= 15 sec), (B) >= 10 simulator takeover sessions per crew with instructor sign-off, (C) full 6-DOF control authority upon takeover.
acceptance_criterion: Crew workload (NASA-TLX) <= 75% nominal, <= 85% contingency in 100% of test flights; situational awareness >= 1 Hz for 100% of duration; override latency < 2 sec for 100% of commands; time-to-takeover within phase limits (VLOS <= 5, BVLOS single <= 10, BVLOS swarm <= 15 sec) for 100% of takeovers; >= 10 documented simulator sessions per crew with instructor sign-off.
reasoning: DeFOSPAM Dorothy-2: 'meaningful human control' lacks measurable criteria. 'Throughout' is temporal ambiguity (no assessment frequency). Workload and takeover thresholds undefined. Chris defines 'meaningful' as 'Active, informed crew awareness and retained authority.' Operationalizing through: (1) frequency metric (1 Hz), (2) override latency (< 2 sec), (3) intervention time window (5-10 sec phase-dependent), (4) workload thresholds (75-85%), (5) measurement (NASA-TLX, simulator, observer). 'Throughout' resolved through per-phase acceptance.
recommendation: For all human-factors requirements, specify: (1) awareness frequency (Hz), (2) authority latency (milliseconds), (3) intervention time windows (seconds, phase-dependent), (4) workload thresholds (% of cognitive capacity), (5) measurement instrument (NASA-TLX, SWAT, etc.). Require simulator practice hours and flight-test observer validation.

finding_title: REQ015: 'Signed apportionment' and 'gap-free regulatory ownership' undefined; no enumeration
original_requirement: As a CAA/MAA liaison I want a signed apportionment so that regulatory ownership is gap-free.
verifiable_rewrite: Regulatory Apportionment Matrix specifies: (1) which requirements (REQ001-REQ020) each authority (CAA or MAA) owns, (2) decision dependencies, (3) information barriers (classified vs. shareable), (4) escalation procedures. Gap-free coverage verified by checklist: all REQ001-REQ020 assigned to CAA, MAA, or jointly. Matrix reviewed and signed by designated CAA and MAA representatives confirming: (A) all 20 requirements assigned, (B) no gaps remain, (C) both authorities acknowledge approval responsibilities and enforcement authority.
acceptance_criterion: 100% of Apportionment Matrices cover all REQ001-REQ020 with no unassigned requirements; 100% signed by designated CAA and MAA representatives; all decision dependencies and information barriers specified; all escalation procedures documented for conflicting requirements; joint oversight mechanisms (shared evidence portal, joint review gates) established.
reasoning: REQ015 uses vague language ('signed apportionment', 'gap-free regulatory ownership'); no definition of what 'gap' means or how to enumerate. Operationalizing through: (1) Apportionment Matrix with 4 columns (requirement, CAA owner, MAA owner, decision dependency), (2) 20-point gap-free checklist (all REQ001-REQ020 assigned), (3) signature requirement by both authorities. This closes governance gap for classified MOD trials.
recommendation: For all regulatory-governance requirements, specify: (1) matrix/checklist format for assignment, (2) enumeration of all requirements covered, (3) signature authority, (4) joint oversight mechanism. Define 'gap-free' explicitly as 100% coverage with no unassigned items.
